## What You Will Learn

A fixed learning rate is rarely optimal. **Learning rate schedulers** automatically decay the LR over time, allowing large steps early (fast convergence) and small steps later (fine-tuning).

### Key Concepts
- **StepLR**: Decays the learning rate by gamma every step_size epochs. Simple but effective.
- **Why Decay?**: Early in training, you need big steps to escape bad local minima. Later, you need small steps to settle into the optimal valley.
- **Other Schedulers**: CosineAnnealingLR (smooth decay), ReduceLROnPlateau (decay when loss stagnates), OneCycleLR (one cycle up then down).

### Why This Matters
Proper LR scheduling can improve final accuracy by several percentage points. It is a standard practice in training state-of-the-art models.
